key_insight: A model-agnostic audit can verify both whether a materials ML model is learning real physics and whether its training labels are correct, using explainability plus targeted DFT checks.
engineering_relevance: For spin Hall conductivity prediction, a composition-only Random Forest reaches competitive accuracy without relaxed structures, enabling screening across many more candidate compositions than structure-dependent models.
method: Use SHAP and counterfactual partial dependence to detect feature–property entanglement.
Apply Rashomon-style cross-model verification to see whether different models agree on the learned signal.
Confirm suspicious findings with targeted first-principles DFT calculations on selected compounds and labels.
evidence_signal: The audit found a Pt-free compound whose true SHC was nearly 4× the model prediction and uncovered a 30× error in the HfC training label.
watch_item: The protocol still depends on a small number of carefully chosen DFT validations, so its reliability hinges on selecting informative audit cases.
paper_title: Auditing Machine-Learning Models and Their Training Data with Explainability and First-Principles Verification: Application to Spin Hall Conductivity
